'''Thomas James Keinhorst''' (born 14 July 1990) is a [[Germany national rugby league team|German]] former international [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|ce}} or {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] and [[Hull KR]] in the [[Super League]], and spent time on loan from [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at the [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], as well as the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the Super League. Keinhorst has also spent time on loan from [[Hull KR]] at the [[York City Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He has played [[rugby union]] for [[Heidelberger RK]] in the German [[Rugby-Bundesliga]] and in England with [[Otley R.U.F.C.|Otley]] in [[National League 1]]. He also represented [[Germany national rugby union team|Germany]] at international level.

==Background==
Keinhorst was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.<ref name="Leeds" >[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/19953.php German international set to make Super League début] Leeds Rhinos website, published: 30 July 2012, accessed: 31 July 2012</ref>

==Playing career==
Keinhorst began playing [[rugby league]] in 2004, while attending [[St Mary's Menston Catholic Voluntary Academy|St. Mary's Catholic High School]].<ref name="Total" >[http://www.totalrugby.de/component/option,com_joomleague/func,showPlayer/p,78/pid,2526/Itemid,385/ James Keinhorst profile at totalrugby.de] {{in lang|de}} accessed: 23 March 2010</ref>

===Playing positions===
====Rugby union====
He primarily played as an [[rugby union positions|inside-centre]], but he could also play as an [[rugby union positions|outside-centre]] or in the [[rugby union positions|second row]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Senior rugby union career===
====Heidelberger RK (2009–2010)====
Keinhorst began playing [[rugby union]] as an [[amateur]] with [[Otley R.U.F.C.|Otley]] and he later moved to Germany to play for [[Heidelberger RK]] in 2009, at the age of 19, and he won the National Championship in the 2009–10 season.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

He played eight games and scored four tries in his first season.

====Otley (2010–2011)====
Keinhorst rejoined [[Otley R.U.F.C.|Otley]] as a [[semi-professional sports|semi-professional]] in 2010, and went onto make 11 appearances in the 2010–11 season, scoring three tries before leaving at the end of the season to sign a [[professional sports|professional]] contract at [[rugby league]] side the [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Senior rugby league career===
====Leeds (2012–2018)====
Keinhorst signed for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 2011, and made his [[Super League]] début on 30 July 2012, in the defeat by the [[Wakefield Trinity| Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]].<ref name="Leeds Rhinos" >[http://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/leeds-rhinos/leeds_rhinos_german_starlet_handed_chance_to_shine_1_4137772 Leeds Rhinos: German starlet handed chance to shine] Yorkshire Evening Post website, accessed: 13 January 2012</ref><ref>[http://www.therhinos.co.uk/news/19958.php Mixed emotions for Keinhorst] Leeds Rhinos website, published: 30 July 2012, accessed: 31 July 2012</ref>

Keinhorst came on as a substitute in [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]' losing effort in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=The Guardian|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web
 |url=http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |title=Warrington Wolves Are Challenge Cup Winners 2012! 
 |date=25 August 2012 
 |work=Warrington Wolves Official Site 
 |access-date=20 November 2019
 |url-status=dead 
 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131217230118/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/warrington-wolves-are-challenge-cup-winners-2012-5628 
 |archive-date=17 December 2013 
}}</ref>

Keinhorst played from the interchange bench for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]], and helped to set-up the winning try for [[Josh Walters]], in a 22–20 victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].
[[File:Jimmy Keinhorst.jpg|thumb|right|Keinhorst playing for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in 2016]]
Keinhorst scored a hat-trick of tries for [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in round 2 of the 2018 [[Super League]] season, against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at [[Elland Road]].

It was revealed at the end of the 2018 campaign, that Keinhorst would be departing [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] to sign a contract to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

====Hull Kingston Rovers (2019–present)====
In October 2018, Keinhorst signed a three-year deal with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45729933|title=Jimmy Keinhorst, Weller Hauraki & Ryan Lannon: Hull Kingston Rovers make triple signing|publisher=BBC Sport|date=3 October 2018|access-date=25 October 2018}}</ref>

On 9 January 2019, Keinhorst received the number 4 jersey ahead of the start of the [[Super League]] season.  On 13 January 2019, Keinhorst made his non-competitive [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' début in a pre-season friendly against [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]], Keinhorst claimed a 30–16 victory with his new club.

Keinhorst made his first competitive appearance for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in round 1 of the 2019 [[Super League]] season, Keinhorst scored a dramatic try in the final six seconds of the match on his début, going onto record a thrilling 18–16 victory over cross-city rivals [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].

On 4 September 2020 it was reported that Keinhorst would resume with Hull KR after his loan period at the [[York City Knights]].<ref>{{cite web|website=loverugbyleague.com|date=4 September 2020|access-date=26 December 2020|title=Jimmy Keinhorst and Rowan Milnes return to Hull KR|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/jimmy-keinhorst-returns-to-hull-kr/}}</ref>

====Castleford (loan)====
On 31 July 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in the [[Super League]] on a short-term loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=The Yorkshire Post|date=31 July 2021|accessdate=1 August 2021|title=Castleford Tigers announce trio of loan signings|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-announce-trio-of-loan-signings-3330205}}</ref>

====Keighley (loan)====
On 24 March 2023, Keinhorst joined Keighley for one match on loan from Hull Kingston Rovers through dual registration.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-jimmy-keinhorst-keighley-8284933/|title=Hull KR's new dual-registration agreement used for the first time as Keighley land Jimmy Keinhorst|website=hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

====York RLFC====
On 13 October 2023, it was reported that he had joined [[York Knights|York RLFC]] for the 2024 RFL Championship on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 October 2023|title=Hull KR utility Jimmy Keinhorst makes permanent Championship move: 'A really important signing for us'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-kr-utility-jimmy-keinhorst-makes-permanent-championship-move-york-knights}}</ref> In August 2024, he announced his retirement from rugby league.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkrlfc.com/post/keinhorst-announces-his-retirement |title=Keinhorst announces his retirement |website=York RLFC |date=8 August 2024 |access-date=14 June 2025}}</ref>

===Representative career===
====Rugby union====
He made his début for [[Germany national rugby union team|Germany]] in a match against [[Poland national rugby union team|Poland]] on 20 November 2010.<ref name="Polen">[http://www.rugby-journal.de/index.php/basis/meldung/auftaktniederlage_fuer_deutsches_nationalteam/ DRV XV: Unnötige Auftaktniederlage gegen Polen]{{dead link|date=April 2017 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }} {{in lang|de}} ''[[Rugby-Journal]]'', published: 21 November 2010, accessed: 21 November 2010</ref>

Keinhorst scored his first try for [[Germany national rugby union team|Germany]] a week later against the [[Netherlands national rugby union team|Netherlands]] in Amsterdam.<ref>[http://www.totalrugby.de/content/view/3428/37/ DRV XV: Deutschland ohne Keinhorst – Hongkong mit Nachwuchsteam] {{in lang|de}} totalrugby.de, published: 11 December 2010, accessed: 11 December 2010</ref>

====Rugby league====
Keinhorst is the youngest of four rugby-playing brothers and qualifies to play for [[Germany national rugby league team|Germany]] through his German father Wolfgang, who has lived in the United Kingdom for many years.<ref>[http://www.wharfedaleobserver.co.uk/sport/4760723.Youngest_Keinhorst_gets_chance_in_Germany/ Youngest Keinhorst gets chance in Germany] ''[[Wharfedale & Airedale Observer]]'', published: 25 November 2009, accessed: 23 November 2010</ref>

Jimmy and his three brothers, [[Kristian Keinhorst|Kristian]], Markus and Nicholas set a world record in 2007, when they played in a [[rugby league]] international game for [[Germany national rugby league team|Germany]] against the [[Czech Republic national rugby league team|Czech Republic]], becoming the most siblings to play in a [[rugby league]] international match.<ref>[http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/news/henderson-kids-make-dad-proud/story-e6freyir-1111114941800 Henderson kids make dad proud] ''[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]'', published: 23 November 2007, accessed: 23 November 2010</ref><ref>[http://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/archive/2010/08/12/Otley+(rugbyunion_otley)/8326384.Otley_hope_skipper_is_not_feeling_under_par/ Otley hope skipper is not feeling under par] ''[[Telegraph & Argus]]'', published: 12 August 2010, accessed: 23 November 2010</ref>

As of 2012, Keinhorst was the top try and point scorer for [[Germany national rugby league team|Germany]].<ref name="League">[http://rugby-league.de/de/spieler/ Nationalmanschafts Satistik 2006–10] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719081350/http://rugby-league.de/de/spieler/ |date=19 July 2011 }} {{in lang|de}} Rugby League Deutschland website, accessed: 11 December 2010</ref>
